content.heading: Give 1 % of your income and **change lives**.
content.description: We send a basic income directly to people in extreme poverty. Transparent, simple and effective.

content.title: It’s not that hard.
content.author: f9a6d2d9-4328-434f-8005-d88df53079e1
content.leadText: Last month, Marc and I went on the biggest adventure of our lives – at least so far. We boarded a flight to Monrovia to help set up a new cash transfer program in Liberia, part of Social Income’s effort to reach more people in West Africa who are living in poverty.
content.subtitle: Here are the basics of basic income.

content.title: Why Global Cash Transfers Matter.
content.author: 7da3f4b9-1176-47f5-acc6-ae15f90e35f5
content.leadText: The Swiss newspaper NZZ proposes a radical idea: a global basic income for the world's poorest people. The reasoning is pragmatic and highly compelling.

image.alt: A child in a red shirt walks a small goat on a leash near a storefront with colorful signs and a parked car.

meta_data.alt: A child in a red shirt walks a small goat on a leash near a storefront with colorful signs and a parked car.

content.title: Why People Leave .
content.author: 1dc9edb3-8741-413f-a86f-4c2dade51c83
content.leadText: In a recent interview, ETH Zurich researcher Jan Freihardt challenged a familiar narrative: that people in the Global South are fleeing their homes en masse toward the Global North due to climate change. It’s an interesting perspective—and one that made me pause.
content.subtitle: And How Cash Lets Them Stay

content.description: At Social Income, committed volunteers give their time to help create lasting change.

content.bio: Willemijn is a Senior IT Business Analyst from the Netherlands, now based in Switzerland. She began her career in law and refugee support and holds master’s degrees in Private Law and in Philosophy of Law and Governance.

content.description: Direct cash programs give money directly to people in need, so they can decide how to use it
Eliminating poverty is at the core of our approach

content.bio: Aurélie writes from Geneva, where she studied international relations at the Graduate Institute. She’s in charge of our monthly newsletter and takes on special missions — like launching new pilots in new countries.
